description:
1908 Lisboa Hotel gives guests a local experience in one of Lisbon’s most up-and-coming neighborhoods. It gains its name from being housed in a completely renovated 1908 award-winning building.

A four-star hotel at a budget price, this elegant space is a perfect base and home to an ever-changing art gallery.

A Cevicheria is fun, and it feels different. There’s a window at the front where those waiting for a table can order drinks from the street. The servers are likely to be dancing to the music in the background. And the food, specifically the puro ceviche served on top of sweet potato and leche de tigre, will leave you speechless.
hubs of the Algarve
This trendy waterfront district is famous for its hopping nightlights and iconic views of the river.

A region in southern Portugal known for its countryside meals, including açorda.

A region in southern Portugal where açorda is a typical countryside meal.
Perched atop Lisbon’s highest hill, this was one of the few areas spared by the earthquake of 1755 and is home to some of the city’s most historic sites.

picture-perfect destination for hiking amongst the dramatic cliffs overlooking the Atlantic and living the good life on the golden beaches

A region in southern Portugal where cozido is often served with mint and potato.

The coast, particularly Nazaré and all along Algarve, is full of locals and visitors alike, as many people are looking to soak up some time in the warm turquoise water
Antiqvvm is a fine dining restaurant serving Mediterranean cuisine using premium seasonal produce, with multi-course tasting menus and a la carte options.

Aveiro, Portugal, otherwise known as the “Venice of Portugal” is an adorable little fishing town about 40 minutes south of Porto. It can be described as overwhelmingly charming and picturesque.

dream destination for photographers, mosaic of gondolas and art nouveau building fill the Ria de Aveiro with color

A city in northern Portugal.
Valverde Hotel is located on Avenida da Liberdade, Lisbon’s most exclusive street.
remote islands made for adventure lovers, famous for its hot springs and whale-watching

The Lumiares is located in Bairro Alto, Lisbon’s cultural and bohemian district.

Just beside Principe Real, you’ll find this lively district.
Famous for its ultra-fresh seafood and colorful tiled houses, this quaint neighborhood will give you a glimpse into the seafaring past.

I’m pretty certain that Bettina & Niccolo Corallo Chocolate Café produces the best chocolate I have ever had. The sorbet is an absolute must, with a texture so airy and a flavor so rich that it feels and tastes like a dark chocolate mousse.
Cafe Guarany is a popular cafe serving coffee in porcelain cups, a perfect spot to enjoy a cup of coffee before heading to the next attraction.
An all-day café serving French-Mediterranean food and healthy vegan options—including brunch faves like shakshuka and banana bread.
live in your bikini

A beach town in southern Portugal.

A city in the Lisbon region of Portugal.

place: Casa de Chá da Boa Nova
description: Casa de Chá da Boa Nova is a restaurant set on the rugged coast, offering incredible ocean views and delicious food, including lobster and Port wine for dessert.
Santiago de Alfama is located near the historical Sao Jorge Castle.

Cervejaria Ramiro is the epitome of simplistic seafood. Everything is absurdly fresh, and also incredibly uncomplicated. Seasoning is key, with most dishes having only butter, garlic, white wine, salt, or pepper as the central flavor.

One of the best places in town for fresh seafood.
In the historic heart of this district is known for its luxury shops, famous landmarks, and historic cafés.
The city park is truly beautiful, perfect for a nice walk in the city center.
Clérigos Tower is a iconic site in Porto, offering breathtaking views of the city from its 250-foot tall tower, with 200 steps to the top.
To get the best of these fall hues, visit the forests of Coimbra

The nearby beach town of Costa Nova is an easy taxi or bus ride away from the city center of Aveiro. If you would like to spend part of your trip in Aveiro at the beach, or experience a drastic change of scenery, then I would recommend that you check out Costa Nova.

stunning beaches

The Costa Verde shoreline is home to beautiful beaches and is a must-visit destination in Northern Portugal.
Crystal Palace Gardens is a picturesque spot to spend a sunny day, with winding paths, fountains, and lush landscape, offering views of the Douro River.

White washed and airy, this is the calming oasis you want to eat a leisurely breakfast in. The dark ceramic plates are gorgeous, and the food is meticulously arranged. Get the scrambled eggs with scallions and black truffles, grab a magazine from the wall, and ease into day one in Lisbon.
An Australian cafe with two locations serving all-day brunch and specialty coffee!
mecca for foodies and wine lovers, UNESCO World Heritage destination
This nineteenth-century Moorish beauty is now a swanky cultural hub.

The salmon tartare in a sort of miso sauce was outstanding, followed by one of the most tender pieces of octopus I’ve ever come across. It was laid on top of a sweet potato mash along with spinach and a light pesto sauce.

For some traditional Portuguese food (vegan-style) then you have to go to Espaco Criativo Biscoito. The owner of this cafe is an amazing human, and she will whip up anything from “Portuguese NON-meatballs” to veggie lasagna to curry dishes!
quaint village with cobbled streets and whitewashed houses
Exmo. Hotel is a modern boutique hotel located in the Norte Region of Porto, within walking distance of Ribeira Square and Palacio da Bolsa.

Fabrica is a funky little space run by a mix of Brazilians, Aussies, and Portuguese. With no available Wi-Fi and hardly any accessible cell service at all, Fabrica is a specialty coffee shop that wants to stay true to just that.
well-preserved heart of this old town has incredible seafood restaurants, hopping nightclubs, and some of the best prices
urban wonders
A mushroom festival is held in Fundao
sip your way around the Green Wine Region

My favorite of all the coffee shops and cafés that I went to, Hello, Kristoff is a small space with a lot of heart. Owned by a coffee aficionado who doubles as a magazine publisher, this shop shows off these combined talents everywhere you look.

Hotel da Baixa offers guests one of the best central locations in Lisbon’s historic district just a short walk from the Santa Justa Elevator and Rossio Square.

A gorgeous four-star option in the heart of the city, this refined property impresses at every turn.
Hotel da Música is a 4-star hotel in the heart of Porto, offering a beautiful contemporary style and on-site restaurant, bar, and 24-hour reception.
surf the waves
got far more to offer than your average beach town
Leitaria da Quinta do Paço is a bakery famous for its eclairs, offering a variety of traditional Portuguese pastries, including pasteis de nata.

Lisbon is the city for you. From historical castles to pristine beaches to fantastic food and wine, Lisbon has something for everyone.

The capital city of Portugal, known for its food scene.

In the spring, you’ll find many celebrations including numerous cheese festivals, a fish festival taking place in Lisbon during April
Lisbon Cheese and Wine Suites is located near Lisbon Cathedral.

Lisbon Cheese and Wine Suites offers guests a variety of rooms from suites to apartments – some including breakfast. Guests are greeted with complimentary cheese and wine upon arrival showcasing some of Lisbon’s finest cuisine.

A well-appointed, family-fun property offers a four-star experience at a great price.
Livraria Lello is a beautiful bookstore, famous for being a favorite haunt of JK Rowling, with a stunning interior and a cover charge to enter.
Honey and chestnut festivals take place in Lousã
The Lx Factory, set beneath the Ponte 25 de Abril (April 25th Bridge), is a combination of photography studios, freelance work spaces, coffee shops, bookstores, and eateries.
live in your bikini
unique wine variety and dreamy tropical climate
region: off the northwest coast of Africa

description: A flower festival in Maderia

Manteigaria is the clear winner for pasteis de nata. Located off a square in Chiado, Manteigaria is a tiny storefront where you can see locals stopping by for a quick espresso, having a chat with a friend, or getting lost watching the production of these ridiculously tasty little treats.
If you would like to continue your cocktail-ing, you can find live music and good vibes at the upstairs bar on the main street known as Mercado Negro, which stays open until 2am.

Located a bit out of the way, or at least outside of the normal tourist circuit in the upscale white-washed Ourique neighborhood, this venue felt warmer and more intimate than the better-know Time Out Market.

The famous Time Out Market is the perfect place to kick off your time in Lisbon. You can grab a coffee and pasteis de nata (egg tart pastries) if you’d like and watch the older generation of Lisbonites pick up its day’s fruits and veggies as the sun is coming up.
Start your day with a delicious brunch, fresh bread, and pão de chocolate from Mila!
Watch the sunset from Miradouro Santa Catarina and soak in the stunning view of the river and the bridge.
To get the best of these fall hues, visit the forests of Montesinho Natural Park
one of the so-called seven wonders of Portugal

A seaside village in western Portugal where polvo a lagareiro is often enjoyed.

The coast, particularly Nazaré and all along Algarve, is full of locals and visitors alike, as many people are looking to soak up some time in the warm turquoise water

beautiful village in the countryside, home to one of the so-called seven wonders of Portugal: the Moorish Castle of Obidos

A chocolate festival in Obidos

Staying in Old Town Lisbon (downtown) is generally the easiest place to stay because it gives you the flexibility to explore Lisbon, but also has access to public transportation which allows you to explore the surrounding areas.
19th-century palace
colorful castle is home to Parque de Pena
People flock to Pasteis de Belem for the famous Portuguese pasteis de nata; the line was wrapped around the corner when I went. Go here just to go, as this is a longstanding Portuguese institution.
epic palace and wander amongst the terraces and courtyards
Ponte de Dom Luis I is a bridge linking Porto and Gaia, offering stunning views of the city and the Douro River, especially during sunset.
A quirky little riverside restaurant, be sure to book ahead—and come for sunset!

A city in northern Portugal known for its Francesinha sandwich and bacalhau à gomes de sá dish.

Head to Porto to sample all the famous Port wine

A city in northern Portugal known for its Francesinha sandwich.

A country known for its seafood and wide variety of spices, with Mediterranean food at its finest.

Portugal is a country that truly has it all—from white sand beaches to architecturally beautiful cities to all the wine you can drink!
A picturesque square, full of lovely little restaurants and cute cafes.
You can spend your winter months skiing in Portugal and then visit Praia dos Pescadores in Algarve
Famous for its striking 19th-century mansions and chic boutiques, this is one of Lisbon’s most-loved neighborhoods.
gothic wonder

Restaurante Esplanada Furnas was a major added bonus to this easy day trip. It offered a view of the Atlantic to die for and some fish to match. The restaurant is built right into the cliffs and the main dining area has only glass walls.
For the ultimate sunset view, visit the rooftop restaurant of Restaurante Mercado Piexe Aveiro.

Restaurante Ze da Mouraria was a total last-minute decision, and it was one of the better meals I had during my time here. It is located in the old Moorish section of Lisbon, and the menu changes daily and serves lunch only.
mosaic of gondolas and art nouveau building fill the Ria de Aveiro with color
Ribeira Neighborhood is a charming area to explore, with a riverfront square, picturesque streets, and plenty of photo opportunities.
remarkably well-preserved ruins from the second century
The Vintage Lisbon is located in Rossio Square.
Hotel da Baixa is located near Rossio Train Station.
Sant Jordi Hostels Lisbon is in the Intendente neighborhood in a beautiful historic building. Guests can take advantage of the fast wifi, guest kitchen, tranquil patio, and on-site bar.
picturesque village
A visit to Santa Joana Museum is a really good activity to go for, and inside you will be able to visit one of the most beautiful churches of Portugal.
Hotel da Baixa is located near the Santa Justa Elevator.

Santiago de Alfama offers guests panoramic views of the city and a short walk to the historical Sao Jorge Castle.

This boutique hotel sits in the historic center of Lisbon and is just an 8-minute walk from the iconic São Jorge Castle.
This is the perfect place to get away from the tourist traps and crowds of the city center.
Selina Porto is a boutique hotel perfect for digital nomads, offering stunning rooms, a restaurant, and a workspace all in one place.
You can spend your winter months skiing in the Serra da Estrela Mountains
To get the best of these fall hues, visit the forests of Serra de Estrela

charming destination in the foothills of a mountain, straight out of a fairytale

Looking for the best time to head to Sintra

Tapisco felt super casual in the best way possible; it’s a no-fuss space with smiling staff you kind of want to be friends with, serving high quality food. Start your night off with the specialty cocktail, the Principe, made with Gin Nordés, Cucumber, Mint, and Vermouth Yzaguirre Blanco.

Tartine was pleasantly surprising, especially its breakfast. Really good sourdough is pretty hard to come by, and Tartine’s excels. This coupled with simple scrambled eggs served as a perfect start to the day.

The Lumiares is a small luxury hotel in Lisbon’s cultural and bohemian district Bairro Alto. Each room is apartment-style and equipped with a kitchenette, oven, and dishwasher.

This stylish, five-star boutique hotel is a quiet escape in the trendy Bairro Alto, with stunning rooftop views.

The Vintage Lisbon is a boutique hotel that celebrates the beauty of Lisbon. Everything in the hotel has been inspired by local Portuguese artisans. Each room comes with its very own bar cart including a complimentary gin and tonic kit.

A five-star spa hotel, perfect for a wellness escape, with a fun retro twist.
The Yeatman is a luxury wine hotel and spa offering breathtaking views of the cityscape and Douro River, with outdoor and indoor infinity swimming pools and a two Michelin star restaurant.
The Yeatman Restaurant is a two Michelin star restaurant offering fine dining experiences with expertly paired wine from Portugal's different regions.
A food hall located in the Mercado da Ribeira at Cais do Sodré!

A region in northeastern Portugal where the Jewish population took refuge and created the dish alheira de mirandela.

A region in northeastern Portugal where the Jewish population took refuge in the late 1400s.

Valverde Hotel allows guests a tranquil stay with a mix of retro details and antiques. Visitors can experience Portuguese cuisine with the daily menu at the on-site restaurant and patio as well as live Fado music on Tuesdays and Saturdays.
Affordable luxury at its best, this five-star, boutique property is easily amongst the poshest spots in the city.
Sant Jordi Hostels Lisbon is located near the Viewpoint of Monte Agudo.
